Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Input A scenario description including database schema claims adjusters claim_events an intended process flow R A E P N C a temporal profile model with specific anomalies R P P N A C E N and a prompt instructing the LLM to identify anomalies generate hypotheses and propose SQL verification queries

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Task Act as the Target LLM Respond to the prompt by identifying anomalies hypothesizing causes and providing SQL queries based on the provided schema and temporal profile Constraint Do not reference the instructions or explanations above Present the content independently

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Deconstruct the Temporal Profile and Anomalies R P 90000 3600 about 25 hours avg 1 hour STDEV Anomaly Low STDEV suggests rigid automated timing or backdating P N 604800 172800 7 days avg 2 days STDEV Anomaly Long delay between approval and notification A C 7200 3600 2 hours avg 1 hour STDEV Anomaly Quick closure after assignment potentially skipping E P steps E N 300 60 5 mins avg 1 min STDEV Anomaly Extremely fast notification after evaluation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Draft Hypotheses R P Low STDEV Could be batch processing SLA automation or data entry errors timestamps copied P N High Avg STDEV Backlog in notification team manual letter generation or system integration failure between approval and notification modules A C Short Fast track claims low value erroneous closure or missing event logs steps happened but were not recorded E N Short Automated notification triggered immediately upon evaluation status change

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Draft SQL Queries PostgreSQL Need to join claim_events to itself or use window functions to calculate time differences between specific activities for the same claim_id Query 1 R P Anomaly Find claims where P minus R is outside expected bounds Query 2 P N Delay Find claims where N minus P is greater than 7 days Query 3 A C Skip Find claims where C minus A is very short and check if E or P exist in between Query 4 Resource Adjuster Correlation Group by resource or join with adjusters to see if specific people or regions correlate with anomalies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refine SQL Syntax Use CTEs or self joins on claim_events Ensure timestamp subtraction works in PostgreSQL returns interval Extract epochs or compare intervals Schema claim_events has claim_id activity timestamp resource claims has claim_type claim_amount adjusters has region specialization

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Construct the Response Section 1 Identified Anomalies Summarize the suspicious pairs from the profile Section 2 Hypotheses Explain why these might happen business logic technical issues fraud Section 3 Verification Queries Provide concrete SQL code blocks with explanations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review against Constraints Did I reference the prompt instructions No Is the tone appropriate Yes analytical and technical Are the SQL queries valid for PostgreSQL Yes using EXTRACT EPOCH FROM or interval comparison

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction during drafting For the R P query since the STDEV is low 1 hour most claims should be within 25 hours plus or minus 3 hours I should query for outliers outside this range or specifically look for the rigidity exact same duration Actually the prompt asks to identify claims where time falls outside expected ranges or correlates with conditions I will focus on finding the outliers that break the pattern or confirm the pattern suspiciously For A C I need to check if E or P occurred between A and C For P N I need to find the long tails

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Let us write the SQL using CTEs for clarity Query Structure WITH activity_times AS SELECT claim_id MAX CASE WHEN activity equals R THEN timestamp END as t_r MAX CASE WHEN activity equals P THEN timestamp END and so on FROM claim_events GROUP BY claim_id This is cleaner than multiple self joins
